demographics.audit_notes: Values align closely with 1990 Decennial Census data for Tract 6.04. Added new fields based on census data.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between the 1990 and 2000 census data points.
Values align closely with 2000 Decennial Census data for Tract 6.04.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between 2000 and 2010 census data. ACS data begins in this period, and trends align.
Values align closely with 2010 Decennial Census and ACS 2006-2010 data for Tract 6.04.
Data reflects a significant demographic shift between 2010 and 2015, consistent with rapid gentrification in East Austin. All values align closely with ACS 5-Year estimates for Census Tract 6.04.
Values align closely with 2020 Decennial Census and ACS 2016-2020 data for Tract 6.04.
Values are plausible estimates based on ACS 2017-2021 data, showing a continuation of the 2015-2020 trend.

property.audit_notes: Property values align with 1990 Census for Tract 6.04. New fields estimated from census and historical city data.
Values are plausible interpolations for the mid-1990s.
Property values align with 2000 Census for Tract 6.04.
Values are plausible interpolations reflecting the pre-recession housing boom.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data for Tract 6.04.
Massive spike in home value is consistent with ACS data and reflects intense gentrification in East Austin during this period.
Continued strong appreciation aligns with ACS and market data for Tract 6.04.
Post-COVID housing boom reflected in sharp value increase, consistent with Zillow and TCAD data. YoY change is an anomaly but reflects market conditions.
Values reflect market peak and subsequent cooling, consistent with city-wide trends.
Slight dip in home value is consistent with market correction in late 2022/early 2023.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census for Tract 6.04. Renamed unemployment field for consistency. Eviction data for this period is sparse and estimated.
Values are plausible interpolations for the mid-1990s.
Values align with 2000 Census for Tract 6.04.
Plausible interpolations. Early signs of gentrification (rising income/education) are visible.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data for Tract 6.04. Significant jump in education level is a key indicator.
Massive increases in income and education align with ACS data, confirming rapid gentrification.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020 data. Unemployment rate reflects pre-COVID peak average for the 5-year period.
Plausible estimates based on ACS 2017-2021 data.
Plausible estimates based on the latest available ACS 2018-2022 data. Eviction rate spike reflects end of pandemic-era moratoriums.
